findings.title: No test for invalid activityType fallback to 'other'
findings.description: The test verifies all 7 valid activity types are passed through correctly, but there is no test for the fallback behavior when the LLM returns an invalid activity type string (e.g., 'gaming' or 'unknown'). The production code at context-merger.ts:183-188 has a validateActivityType method that defaults to 'other' for unrecognized types. This fallback logic is untested and could silently break if refactored.

findings.title: computeImportance clamp logic not tested with out-of-range values
findings.description: The test named 'clamps result to [0, 1]' only tests with valid boundary values (0 and 1). It does not test values outside the valid range (e.g., importance: 1.5 or importance: -0.3) that would actually exercise the Math.max(0, Math.min(1, ...)) clamping logic in computeImportance (context-merger.ts:80). The ContextSource type allows importance as an optional number with no range constraint, so out-of-range values are possible at runtime.

findings.title: StubLlmProvider routes by schema content string matching -- brittle
findings.description: The StubLlmProvider.generateStructured method routes its response by checking if schemaDescription.includes('persons'). This couples the test to an internal implementation detail (the exact schema description string). If the schema description in entity-extractor.ts or context-merger.ts changes (e.g., renaming 'persons' to 'people' or restructuring the schema format), the stub will silently return the wrong response type, causing confusing test failures or false passes.

positive_observations: Pure functions (deduplicateStrings, mergeKeywords, computeImportance, selectTopSources) are cleanly separated as Functional Core and tested without any mocks -- direct input/output testing.
Test doubles for external LLM API follow the project convention with '// Test Double rationale:' comments and implement the LlmProvider interface rather than using vi.fn() or vi.mock().
Good error path coverage: both ContextMerger and EntityExtractor test LLM failure scenarios with proper error wrapping (cause chaining) verification.
Edge cases for empty inputs are consistently tested across utility functions (empty arrays for deduplicateStrings, mergeKeywords, selectTopSources, computeImportance).
The context-merger-utils.test.ts file properly tests the Functional Core in isolation from the Imperative Shell, following the architecture convention.
No vi.fn(), vi.mock(), jest.fn(), jest.mock(), InMemoryRepository, MockXxx, FakeXxx, or skipped tests detected.
